evals.prompt: I get support tickets via a webhook and need to process them in three steps: classify the ticket (billing, technical, account), pull relevant docs from our help center, then draft a response using the classification and docs. About 100 labeled examples. Using GPT-4o-mini.
evals.expected_output: A DSPy Module with three sub-modules wired together in forward(): a classifier, a retriever, and a response drafter. Each step should have its own Signature. Should show how to optimize the full pipeline end-to-end with a single metric, not each stage separately.

assertions.name: defines_pipeline_module
assertions.description: Creates a dspy.Module subclass that composes multiple sub-modules in __init__

assertions.name: has_separate_signatures
assertions.description: Defines distinct dspy.Signature classes for classification and response drafting

assertions.name: wires_in_forward
assertions.description: The forward() method chains outputs from earlier stages as inputs to later stages

assertions.name: end_to_end_optimization
assertions.description: Uses a DSPy optimizer (BootstrapFewShot or MIPROv2) on the full pipeline, not individual modules

assertions.name: includes_retrieval
assertions.description: Includes a retrieval step (dspy.Retrieve or similar) between classification and generation

evals.prompt: I want to build a content pipeline that takes a rough draft blog post, checks it for factual claims, looks up sources for each claim, then rewrites the draft with citations. The tricky part is the verification step might find some claims are wrong and those need to be flagged or removed, not just cited.
evals.expected_output: A multi-stage DSPy pipeline with generate/extract claims, verify claims, and rewrite stages. Should handle the branching case where unverifiable claims are flagged. Each stage is its own module with a clear signature.

assertions.name: defines_pipeline_module
assertions.description: Creates a dspy.Module that composes claim extraction, verification, and rewriting sub-modules

assertions.name: handles_branching
assertions.description: Includes conditional logic in forward() to handle verified vs unverifiable claims differently

assertions.name: has_verification_stage
assertions.description: Includes a distinct verification or fact-checking module with its own signature

assertions.name: has_rewrite_stage
assertions.description: Includes a rewriting module that takes verified claims and produces cited output

assertions.name: provider_agnostic
assertions.description: Any dspy.LM() call includes a provider-alternative comment

evals.prompt: We have a customer onboarding flow where we need to: 1) extract key info from a signup form (company name, industry, size), 2) classify them into a tier (starter, growth, enterprise), 3) generate a personalized welcome email based on their tier. I want to use a cheap model for extraction and classification but a better model for the email. No labeled data yet.
evals.expected_output: A DSPy pipeline with three stages using different models per stage. Should show how to assign cheap_lm to extraction/classification modules and expensive_lm to the email generator. Should address the cold-start scenario.

assertions.name: defines_pipeline_module
assertions.description: Creates a dspy.Module composing extraction, classification, and generation sub-modules

assertions.name: assigns_different_models
assertions.description: Shows how to assign different dspy.LM instances to different stages of the pipeline

assertions.name: has_extraction_stage
assertions.description: Includes a module that extracts structured fields from the signup form text

assertions.name: handles_cold_start
assertions.description: Addresses the no-labeled-data scenario with zero-shot approach or synthetic data suggestion

assertions.name: generates_personalized_email
assertions.description: Final stage generates a welcome email conditioned on the extracted tier and company info
